ddls:
CREATE TABLE "films" (
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each film */,
  "original_title" TEXT /* Title of the film as originally released */,
  "overview" TEXT /* Summary of the film's plot */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the film was released */,
  "runtime" INTEGER /* Duration of the film in minutes */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget allocated for the film production */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Total revenue generated by the film */,
  "tagline" TEXT /* Catchphrase or slogan associated with the film */,
  "original_language" TEXT /* Language in which the film was originally produced */,
  "imdb_id" TEXT /* IMDB ID of the film */,
  "tmdb_id" TEXT /* TMDB ID of the film */,
  "poster_path" TEXT /* Path to the film's poster */,
  "backdrop_path" TEXT /* Path to the film's backdrop image */,
  "production_status" TEXT /* Production status of the film (e.g., Released, Post Production) */,
  "imdb_rating" REAL /* IMDB rating of the film */,
  "tmdb_rating" REAL /* TMDB rating of the film */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "casts" (
  "cast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the cast entry */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film this cast member is associated with */,
  "character_name" TEXT /* Character played by the actor in the film */,
  "actor_name" TEXT /* Name of the actor portraying the character */,
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the actor */,
  "cast_order" INTEGER /* Order of the cast member in the credits */,
  "character_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the character */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_casts_actor_id FOREIGN KEY ("actor_id") REFERENCES actors ("actor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_casts_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actors" (
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the actor */,
  "actor_name" TEXT /* Name of the actor */,
  "birth_date" TEXT /* Birth date of the actor */,
  "death_date" TEXT /* Death date of the actor (if applicable) */,
  "biography" TEXT /* Biography of the actor */,
  "profile_path" TEXT /* Path to the actor's profile image */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("actor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "crews" (
  "crew_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the crew entry */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film this crew member is associated with */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Title of the job (e.g., Director, Producer) */,
  "crew_member" TEXT /* Name of the crew member in that role */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department in which the crew member worked (e.g., Production, Sound) */,
  "crew_member_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the crew member */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("crew_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crews_crew_member_id FOREIGN KEY ("crew_member_id") REFERENCES crew_members ("crew_member_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crews_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "crew_members" (
  "crew_member_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the crew member */,
  "crew_member_name" TEXT /* Name of the crew member */,
  "birth_date" TEXT /* Birth date of the crew member */,
  "death_date" TEXT /* Death date of the crew member (if applicable) */,
  "biography" TEXT /* Biography of the crew member */,
  "profile_path" TEXT /* Path to the crew member's profile image */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("crew_member_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the genre entry */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film this genre is associated with */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre (e.g., Action, Adventure) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_genres_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "keywords" (
  "keyword_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the keyword entry */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film this keyword is associated with */,
  "keyword" TEXT /* Keyword describing a theme or element of the film */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("keyword_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_keywords_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "production_companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the production company entry */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film produced by this company */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the production company */,
  "company_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the production company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_production_companies_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "production_countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the production country entry */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film produced in this country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code for the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_production_countries_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "languages" (
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the language entry */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film this language is associated with */,
  "language_name" TEXT /* Name of the spoken language */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO 639-1 code for the language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_languages_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each review entry */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film being reviewed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who wrote the review */,
  "review_text" TEXT /* Text content of the review */,
  "vote_average" REAL /* Average rating given by the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the review was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the review was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user (hashed) */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., Admin, User) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "box_office" (
  "box_office_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the box office entry */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film for which the box office is logged */,
  "domestic_gross" REAL /* Domestic gross revenue */,
  "international_gross" REAL /* International gross revenue */,
  "total_gross" REAL /* Total gross revenue */,
  "opening_weekend" REAL /* Opening weekend revenue */,
  "opening_weekend_date" TEXT /* Date of the opening weekend */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("box_office_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_box_office_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film being rated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who provided the rating */,
  "rating_value" REAL /* Value of the rating given by the user */,
  "rated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the rating was given */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "film_relationships" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the film relationship entry */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film in the relationship */,
  "related_film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the related film */,
  "relationship_type" TEXT /* Type of relationship (e.g., sequel, remake, spin-off) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_film_relationships_related_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("related_film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_film_relationships_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)
